Politics.headline: UK's Labour set for heavy losses in elections as Reform makes early gains
Politics.detail: Early results indicate the Labour Party is facing significant losses in the UK elections, while the Reform party is gaining ground, potentially reshaping the political landscape. The results put Keir Starmer's leadership in question.

Politics.headline: US, Iran clash in Hormuz as war escalates: What happened, why it matters
Politics.detail: Tensions between the US and Iran have escalated with clashes reported in the Strait of Hormuz, a critical waterway, raising concerns about regional stability and the ongoing conflict. The clash occurred amid ceasefire tensions.

Politics.headline: Trump says ceasefire still intact after burst of violence with Iran
Politics.detail: Despite recent violence with Iran, former President Trump claims the ceasefire agreement remains in effect. The attacks cast fresh doubt on efforts by Washington and Tehran to reach a negotiated settlement that would end hostilities.

Politics.headline: Brazilian officials are hoping for the best outcome from the meeting, given tensions over Trump's tariff policy.
Politics.detail: Brazil's Lula meets with Trump amid efforts to avert new US trade tariffs. Brazilian officials are hoping for the best outcome from the meeting, given tensions over Trump's tariff policy.

Politics.headline: Environmental law changes 'possible' to expedite resource projects: government House leader
Politics.detail: The Carney government is planning changes to speed up approvals for pipelines and resource projects. Environmental law changes are 'possible' to expedite resource projects, according to the government House leader.

Finance & Economy.headline: US added 115,000 jobs in April in surprise gain amid Iran war uncertainty
Finance & Economy.detail: The US economy added 115,000 jobs in April, exceeding expectations despite economic uncertainties related to the Iran war, with employment rising in sectors like healthcare and transportation. This marks the first back-to-back monthly gain in nearly a year, although there were declines in federal government employment.

Finance & Economy.headline: Canada's unemployment rate rises to six-month high as full-time jobs drop
Finance & Economy.detail: Canada's unemployment rate increased to 6.9% in April, the highest in six months, as the economy experienced a net loss of 17,700 jobs, indicating continued weakness in the labor market due to U.S. tariffs and trade uncertainties. Statistics Canada's data released on Friday highlights the struggles the Canadian labor market is facing.

Finance & Economy.headline: US petrol prices 50 percent higher than before war on Iran
Finance & Economy.detail: The average price of gasoline in the United States has reached $4.48 per gallon, a 50 percent increase since the beginning of the war on Iran. Surging jet fuel prices are also causing airlines to cut flight schedules, impacting summer travel.

Finance & Economy.headline: Forex reserves fall by $7.79 billion to $690.69 billion: RBI data
Finance & Economy.detail: India's forex reserves have decreased by $7.79 billion, bringing the total to $690.69 billion, according to recent data from the Reserve Bank of India. This decline reflects changes in the country's external financial position.

Finance & Economy.headline: Real GDP increased 2.0 percent in the first quarter of 2026
Finance & Economy.detail: According to the advance estimate by the U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis, the real gross domestic product (GDP) in the U.S. increased at an annual rate of 2.0 percent in the first quarter of 2026. The increase was driven by investment, exports, consumer spending, and government spending.

Technology.headline: Cyberattack hits Canvas system used by thousands of schools as finals loom
Technology.detail: A cyberattack has caused chaos for students at thousands of schools as they study for finals, targeting the system that manages grades, assignments, and lecture videos. The attack occurred on Thursday, causing widespread disruption.

Technology.headline: AI tool unifies fragmented cell maps into spatial atlases across tissues
Technology.detail: A new AI tool is helping to unify fragmented cell maps into spatial atlases across various tissues, potentially enhancing research and understanding in the field. This advancement allows for a more cohesive view of cellular structures and their functions.

Technology.headline: Tech launches today (May 8, 2026): OnePlus Nord CE6 goes on sale, Samsung launches Mini LED TVs, and more
Technology.detail: Today's tech launches include AI-powered Mini LED TVs, flip smartphones, gaming-focused devices, and smart wearables, offering advancements in performance and design. Key releases feature the OnePlus Nord CE6 and Samsung's new Mini LED TV lineup.

Technology.headline: Google Debuts Fitbit Air Screen-less Health Tracker Wearable That Promises Comfort, Simplicity
Technology.detail: Google has launched the Fitbit Air, a screen-less health tracker wearable that prioritizes comfort and simplicity, aiming to provide users with an easy way to monitor their health metrics. This new device offers a streamlined approach to fitness tracking.

Technology.headline: Cyble Positioned as a Challenger in the 2026 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Cyberthreat Intelligence Technologies
Technology.detail: Cyble has been recognized as a Challenger in the 2026 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Cyberthreat Intelligence Technologies, highlighting its AI-native capabilities that enable proactive, intelligence-driven defense. This recognition underscores Cyble's mission to deliver actionable threat intelligence.

Science.headline: Emperor penguins are on the march — toward extinction
Science.detail: The Antarctic birds are now endangered.

Science.headline: Hantavirus cruise LIVE: Cruise passengers monitored in several US states as CDC sets lowest emergency response
Science.detail: Passengers on a recent cruise are being monitored across several US states after a hantavirus outbreak, with the CDC setting its lowest level of emergency response.

Science.headline: A Sleep Disorder Affecting Nearly 1 Billion People Is Still Overlooked
Science.detail: A sleep disorder impacting nearly 1 billion individuals remains largely unaddressed.

Science.headline: Neuroscience. 25 people learned to fly with virtual wings. Here's how the brain changed.
Science.detail: A study explored how the brain adapts when individuals learn to fly using virtual wings, revealing changes in neural pathways.

Science.headline: The '100-Day Cough' Often Missed in Adults Is Highly Contagious
Science.detail: A highly contagious respiratory illness, often overlooked in adults, is identified as the '100-Day Cough.'

Health.headline: Common Constipation Drug May Slow Chronic Kidney Disease
Health.detail: Researchers have found that lubiprostone, a drug typically used for constipation, may offer a new approach to slowing chronic kidney disease, which affects millions and often leads to dialysis. A clinical trial with 150 patients showed promising results.

Health.headline: Interrupting Bacterial Communication in the Mouth
Health.detail: Scientists have discovered that influencing how bacteria in our mouths communicate, rather than killing them, could be a new approach to oral health. This involves interrupting their 'talk' to each other.

Health.headline: More States, Countries On Alert For Hantavirus
Health.detail: Following a hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship, multiple states and countries are on alert, with the WHO urging people to remain calm and emphasizing that it is not COVID. The cruise ship is heading to the Canary Islands for screening.

Health.headline: Medicare To Launch Pilot GLP-1 Drug Program For $50 A Month
Health.detail: Medicare is set to launch a pilot program offering GLP-1 drugs for $50 a month. This initiative aims to provide more affordable access to these medications.

Health.headline: Ovarian Reserve Testing May Mislead Fertility Assessment
Health.detail: According to Medscape Medical News, Ovarian reserve testing may mislead fertility assessment. This suggests a need for caution when interpreting these tests in fertility planning.

Sports.headline: Miami host committee assured ICE will not be at World Cup games
Sports.detail: The Miami host committee has received assurances that ICE (Immigration and Customs Enforcement) will not be present at World Cup games held in Miami.

Sports.headline: Thunder pull away from Lakers in NBA playoffs, while Pistons down Cavs
Sports.detail: The Oklahoma City Thunder defeated the Los Angeles Lakers in the NBA playoffs, and the Detroit Pistons defeated the Cleveland Cavaliers.

Sports.headline: NBA Playoffs: Knicks' OG Anunoby questionable for Game 3
Sports.detail: OG Anunoby's availability for Game 3 of the NBA Playoffs is uncertain.

Sports.headline: NBA: Hornets give coach Charles Lee a contract extension
Sports.detail: The Charlotte Hornets have extended the contract of their head coach, Charles Lee.

Sports.headline: Yankees' Jasson Domínguez has left shoulder sprain after crashing into left-field wall in 1st inning
Sports.detail: Jasson Domínguez, the left fielder for the New York Yankees, suffered a left shoulder sprain after colliding with the wall while attempting to catch a ball in the first inning against Texas.

Entertainment.headline: Max Collins pushes back against 'losyang mom' stigma: 'Moms can be hot!'
Entertainment.detail: Filipina actress Max Collins speaks out against societal pressures on mothers to look and feel a certain way, advocating for self-love and embracing motherhood without sacrificing personal identity.

Entertainment.headline: Bea Millan-Windorski felt 'rejected' by both Americans, Filipinos growing up
Entertainment.detail: Bea Millan-Windorski shares her experiences growing up feeling disconnected from both American and Filipino cultures, highlighting the challenges of navigating identity as a biracial individual.

Entertainment.headline: Rapper Kodak Black arrested in Florida on drug trafficking charge, pleads not guilty
Entertainment.detail: Rapper Kodak Black was arrested in Florida on drug trafficking charges and has pleaded not guilty; further details on the arrest and legal proceedings are emerging.

Entertainment.headline: Renee Zellweger, Ant Anstead's Guest Seeking $10M
Entertainment.detail: A guest at Renee Zellweger and Ant Anstead's home is reportedly seeking $10 million, though details surrounding the situation are currently unclear.

Entertainment.headline: Kodak Black Arrested for Drug Trafficking
Entertainment.detail: Rapper Kodak Black has been arrested on drug trafficking charges, adding to his history of legal issues.

Crime & Justice.headline: Cyberattack hits Canvas system used by thousands of schools as finals loom
Crime & Justice.detail: A cyberattack has caused chaos for students at thousands of schools as they study for finals, targeting a system used to manage grades, assignments and lecture videos.

Crime & Justice.headline: Ex-deputy found guilty of reckless homicide in shooting of Black man entering grandmother's home
Crime & Justice.detail: A former Ohio deputy was found guilty of reckless homicide in the shooting of Casey Goodson Jr.

Crime & Justice.headline: Rapper Kodak Black arrested in Florida on felony MDMA trafficking charge and pleads not guilty
Crime & Justice.detail: Kodak Black was arrested in Florida on a felony MDMA trafficking charge and has pleaded not guilty.

Crime & Justice.headline: French prosecutors seek charges against Elon Musk and X over child sexual abuse images
Crime & Justice.detail: French prosecutors are seeking charges against Elon Musk and X (formerly Twitter) regarding child sexual abuse images on the platform.

Crime & Justice.headline: Cofounder of British fashion brand Superdry sentenced to 8 years in prison for rape
Crime & Justice.detail: James Holder, the co-founder of British fashion brand Superdry, has been sentenced to 8 years in prison for rape.

Education.headline: EMSB honours three 'outstanding' teachers
Education.detail: Three teachers are being recognized for their dedication and impact on students at this year's EMSB Teacher Recognition Awards, with a celebration taking place on Friday at Laurier Macdonald High School.

Education.headline: Cyberattack hits Canvas system used by thousands of schools as finals loom
Education.detail: Canvas, a system used by thousands of schools and universities, experienced a cyberattack on Thursday, disrupting students' ability to study for finals, with a hacking group named ShinyHunters claiming responsibility.

Education.headline: Maharashtra SSC Re-evaluation 2026 Starts Tomorrow: Verification, Photocopy Rules Explained
Education.detail: Maharashtra State Board of Secondary and Higher Secondary Education (MSBSHSE) is set to commence the re-evaluation process for Secondary School Certificate (SSC) Class 10th, exams, providing students with an opportunity to verify and obtain photocopies of their answer sheets.

Education.headline: Education, not concrete or steel, will build Bangladesh-China bridge: Milon
Education.detail: Education and Primary and Mass Education Minister Dr ANM Ehsanul Hoque Milon stated that education, rather than physical materials, will form the foundation for stronger relations between Bangladesh and China, as they enhance cooperation in higher education, governance, and development.

Education.headline: Free meals in further education guide
Education.detail: The Department for Education has released guidance on free meals in further education.

Lifestyle.headline: Small-group meet-ups, shared interests: How young women in Singapore are finding new social circles
Lifestyle.detail: From pilates to supper clubs, female-led social groups are offering young women in Singapore new, low-pressure, activity-based ways to meet and build friendships.

Lifestyle.headline: From cramped coach house to family home – how clever design transformed this tiny space
Lifestyle.detail: A cleverly designed coach house was transformed into a functional family home.

Lifestyle.headline: Cocktail of the week: Le Magritte's bitter velvet – recipe
Lifestyle.detail: A new cocktail recipe is featured, called Le Magritte's bitter velvet.

Lifestyle.headline: Kris Jenner Reveals Why She Quit Ozempic: 'Made Me Sick And Nauseous'
Lifestyle.detail: Kris Jenner revealed she quit taking Ozempic because it made her sick and nauseous.

Lifestyle.headline: 3 evacuated off cruise ship with suspected hantavirus cluster: WHO
Lifestyle.detail: Three people were evacuated from a cruise ship due to a suspected hantavirus cluster, according to the WHO.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Britain's fiscal warning to America
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Investors are growing wary about the U.K.'s ability to manage its growing debts as its long-term borrowing costs have risen to their highest level in 28 years, serving as a warning to America about reckless spending habits. This situation is partly blamed on the Iran war and Britain's limited access to its own oil reserves.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The Comey indictment and free speech
Opinion/Editorial.detail: An editorial discusses the indictment of James Comey for an Instagram post, arguing that it chills free speech and that speech should be judged in the marketplace of ideas, not by government approval. The article also references the Supreme Court's stance on protecting even offensive speech unless it incites violence and emphasizes the importance of interpreting ambiguous speech in favor of the defendant.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: A student's case to change school start times
Opinion/Editorial.detail: A high school student argues for later school start times, citing medical research that shows sleep-deprived teenagers struggle academically and are more prone to car accidents, depression, and anxiety. The primary obstacle to change is bus schedules, but the long-term economic benefits of later start times outweigh the costs.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Are we rolling out AI laws too soon?
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Jim Morrissey discusses the potential issues of implementing AI laws prematurely. The Colorado Sun is a nonpartisan news organization, and the opinions of columnists and editorial writers do not reflect the opinions of the newsroom.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Editorial: Goldfish invasion damages ecosystem
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The Toledo Blade discusses how released goldfish are harming local ecosystems. The Ohio Department of Natural Resources suggests larger tanks or returning unwanted goldfish to pet stores.

World.headline: US, Iran clash in Hormuz as war escalates: What happened, why it matters
World.detail: The US and Iran traded attacks over the Strait of Hormuz, with both sides blaming each other for breaking a month-old truce, escalating the ongoing conflict.

World.headline: Federal court blocks Trump's global tariffs
World.detail: A US federal court overruled President Donald Trump's latest effort to impose new global tariffs, stating his justification for circumventing a previous Supreme Court block was inadequate.

World.headline: Ongoing killing and displacement of women and girls in Lebanon under a fragile ceasefire
World.detail: Despite a ceasefire in Lebanon, the killing and displacement of women and girls continue, with 25 women reported killed and 109 injured in the past three weeks, leading to decreased food availability and eroded hope.

World.headline: Belgium and WHO launch new collaboration as country continues to strengthen action on youth nicotine addiction
World.detail: Belgium and the World Health Organization signed a new Country Cooperation Strategy for 2026–2030, focusing on strengthening public health and addressing youth nicotine addiction by banning flavored e-cigarettes by September 2028.

World.headline: World shares fall and oil prices dip after attacks imperil the ceasefire with Iran
World.detail: World shares retreated and oil prices fell as the fragile ceasefire with Iran was strained by missile and drone attacks, prompting US retaliatory strikes, though President Trump maintains the ceasefire is intact.

Zimbabwe.headline: Govt finalises arrears, debt clearance roadmap
Zimbabwe.detail: The government of Zimbabwe is finalizing a roadmap for arrears and debt clearance, aiming to address the country's debt burden and improve its financial standing.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zim walks talk on re-engagement
Zimbabwe.detail: Zimbabwe is reportedly making progress in its re-engagement efforts with the international community, signaling a potential shift in diplomatic relations and economic cooperation.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zimbabwe shines at Passport DC, eyes growing US tourism market
Zimbabwe.detail: Zimbabwe is promoting its tourism sector at Passport DC, an event in Washington D.C., with the aim of attracting more tourists from the United States.

Zimbabwe.headline: Harare's Sally Mugabe Hospital records the highest births in Zimbabwe with 22 000 baby deliveries
Zimbabwe.detail: Sally Mugabe Hospital in Harare has reported a significant number of births, highlighting its role as a major healthcare provider in the country.

Zimbabwe.headline: Govt declares Mutoko accident State of Disaster
Zimbabwe.detail: Following a fatal accident in Mutoko, the government has declared the incident a State of Disaster, likely to allocate resources for assistance and support to the affected families and community.
